sect4.title: AAP Coding Assistance and Education
sect4.para: AAP Payer Advocacy: Prepayment Downloading Checklist (https://downloads.aap.org/AAP/PDF/Downcoding%20Checklist.pdf) Pediatric ICD-10-CM 2026: A Manual for Provider-Based Coding (www.aap.org/Pediatric-ICD-10-CM-2026-11th-Edition-Paperback)

sect4.title: AAP Pediatric Coding Newsletter™
sect4.para: "Coding for Conditions Due to Heat Overexposure," July 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book243 document001) "Coding Hotline," April 2025 (CPT: Prescription Drug Management) (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book252 document005)

"Coding Hotline," October 2024 (CPT: Emergency Department Observation) (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book246 document006) "Connecting Coding and Clinical Reports: Oral and Dental Aspects of Child Abuse and Neglect," February 2025 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book250 document001)

"Critical Care: What's Included? What's Not?" June 2025 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book254 document004) "Learning Through Examples: Coding for Concerns Related to Child Abuse or Neglect," February 2025 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book250 document005)

"Procedural Services: Modifiers to Report Site and Laterality," January 2025 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book249 document005) "You Code It! Cannabis Poisoning in a 4-Year-Old," April 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book240 document005)

"You Code It! Answers: Cannabis Poisoning in a 4-Year-Old," April 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book240 document006) ● indicates a new code; ▲, revised; †, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.
"Asthma, Head Injury, and Laceration: Coding Continuums"

para.emphasis: Which of the following must be designated in the documentation of an ED visit to help support the urgency of the ED visit under prudent layperson guidelines?

listitem.para: 1 sign, symptom, disease, or injury as the chief complaint
All signs and symptoms noted during triage
1 definitive diagnosis (ie, not a sign or symptom)
A diagnosis that typically requires at least moderate MDM

para.emphasis: Which service is appropriately reported with Z04.72, encounter for examination and observation following alleged child physical abuse?

listitem.para: A visit at which child physical abuse is suspected but not confirmed.
A visit at which child physical abuse is confirmed.
A visit at which a child's signs or symptoms of illness or injury are addressed and determined to not bedue to abuse.
A visit at which the child, who presents without signs or symptoms, is observed for alleged child abuse that is ruled out at the visit.

para.emphasis: Which of the following is the starting point for initial hospital inpatient or observation services (99221–99223) initiated by an ED physician or QHP?

listitem.para: The time at which the patient presented to the ED facility for care
The time at which the ED physician or QHP first provided face-to-face care to the patient
The time at which the ED physician or QHP wrote an order for the patient's admission to observation status
Time beyond the first hour spent in the ED

para.emphasis: Which procedure would not be reported by a physician or QHP providing care in the ED?

listitem.para: General supervision of services performed by hospital personnel
Hydration, infusion, and/or injection procedures
Procedures involving use of hospital-owned equipment and have no associated professional component that would be reported with modifier 26
All of the above

para.emphasis: Per CPT instruction, what is the minimum time of critical care service required to report 99292 in addition to 99291?

listitem.para: 104 minutes
61 minutes
75 minutes
89 minutes
